title: ROUTER block content
description: Cascade policy carried by a `ROUTER` PipelineBlock. The classifier-router reads this block to decide which tier (BERT → SLM → LLM) handles each request and at what threshold. Per architecture §3 / §8.1 and CSV #2.

tiers.description: Per-tier acceptance thresholds + enabled flags. Confidence ≥ accept short-circuits the cascade and the tier becomes `tierOfDecision` on the response.

fallback.description: What the router does when every enabled tier falls through (confidence below its accept threshold) or every tier errors out.

strategy.description: `LLM_FLOOR`: return the LLM tier's result even if it didn't clear its threshold (the historical behaviour — LLM is the cascade's floor). `ROUTER_SHORT_CIRCUIT`: return the configured `defaultResult` without invoking any model. Used when every tier is intentionally disabled.

defaultResult.description: Block-shaped fallback payload returned when `strategy=ROUTER_SHORT_CIRCUIT`. The router doesn't constrain the shape — that belongs to the consuming PROMPT / BERT_CLASSIFIER block.

categoryOverrides.description: Per-category overrides. Key matches `result.categoryId` produced by an upstream tier; the override applies for the next stage (e.g. PII / PHI / PCI scans + metadata extraction triggered by the same router for that category).

costBudget.description: Optional cost ceiling per cascade run.

maxCostUnits.description: Stop the cascade if the next tier's cost would push the running total past this. CSV #22 unit (1024 source bytes ≈ 1 unit).

enabled.description: When `false`, the router skips this tier entirely. Used to disable a tier without removing it from the cascade definition.

accept.description: Confidence threshold this tier must clear to short-circuit the cascade. Set `> 1.0` (e.g. `1.01`) to functionally disable acceptance — every result falls through to the next tier. Phase 1.2 default seeds use `1.01` everywhere so the cascade is conservative until tuning lands.

modelRef.description: Optional pointer to a model artefact the tier should use. For BERT this is a MinIO path to an ONNX model (per Phase 1.4). For SLM / LLM it's a model id (e.g. `claude-haiku-4-5`).

timeout.description: ISO-8601 duration. Tier-specific dispatch timeout; absent means use the router's global default.
